# Hook Presets

Stack-specific presets for the Guardrails & Automation interview domain. Organized into two sections: **Protection Presets** (PreToolUse, block edits to sensitive files) and **Automation Presets** (PostToolUse, run formatters/linters after edits).

---

# Protection Presets (PreToolUse)

## General (always included)

These presets apply to every project regardless of stack.

| Pattern | Description | Recommended |
|---|---|---|
| `.git/` | Git internals ‚Äî never edit directly | Yes |
| `*.key` / `*.pem` | Cryptographic keys and certificates | Yes |
| `.claude/settings.json` | Claude Code configuration ‚Äî prevent accidental overwrites | Yes |

### MultiSelect Format

```
[x] .git/ ‚Äî Git internals (never edit directly)
[x] *.key / *.pem ‚Äî Cryptographic keys and certificates
[x] .claude/settings.json ‚Äî Claude config (prevent accidental overwrites)
```

---

## Python

Apply when the detected or stated language is Python.

| Pattern | Description | Recommended |
|---|---|---|
| `.env` | Environment variables ‚Äî often contains secrets, API keys, database credentials | Yes |
| `uv.lock` | uv lock file ‚Äî auto-generated by `uv lock`, do not edit manually | Yes |
| `poetry.lock` | Poetry lock file ‚Äî auto-generated by `poetry lock`, do not edit manually | Yes |
| `migrations/` | Database migration files ‚Äî require manual review and sequential ordering | Yes |
| `__pycache__/` | Compiled Python bytecode ‚Äî auto-generated, never edit | Yes |
| `.venv/` | Virtual environment directory ‚Äî auto-generated, never edit | Yes |
| `alembic/versions/` | Alembic migration versions ‚Äî require manual review | No |

### MultiSelect Format

```
[x] .env ‚Äî Environment variables with secrets
[x] uv.lock ‚Äî Lock file (auto-generated by uv)
[x] poetry.lock ‚Äî Lock file (auto-generated by Poetry)
[x] migrations/ ‚Äî Database migrations (manual review required)
[x] __pycache__/ ‚Äî Compiled bytecode (auto-generated)
[x] .venv/ ‚Äî Virtual environment (auto-generated)
[ ] alembic/versions/ ‚Äî Alembic migrations (manual review required)
```

### Detection Hints

- Show `uv.lock` only if `uv.lock` or `pyproject.toml` with `[tool.uv]` exists
- Show `poetry.lock` only if `poetry.lock` or `pyproject.toml` with `[tool.poetry]` exists
- Show `migrations/` only if a `migrations/` directory exists or Django/Alembic is detected
- Always show `.env`, `__pycache__/`, `.venv/`

---

## JavaScript / TypeScript

Apply when the detected or stated language is JavaScript or TypeScript.

| Pattern | Description | Recommended |
|---|---|---|
| `.env` | Environment variables ‚Äî often contains secrets, API keys, database credentials | Yes |
| `node_modules/` | npm/yarn/pnpm dependencies ‚Äî auto-installed, never edit | Yes |
| `package-lock.json` | npm lock file ‚Äî auto-generated by `npm install`, do not edit manually | Yes |
| `yarn.lock` | Yarn lock file ‚Äî auto-generated by `yarn install`, do not edit manually | Yes |
| `pnpm-lock.yaml` | pnpm lock file ‚Äî auto-generated by `pnpm install`, do not edit manually | Yes |
| `dist/` | Build output directory ‚Äî auto-generated by bundler | Yes |
| `build/` | Build output directory ‚Äî auto-generated by bundler | Yes |
| `.next/` | Next.js build cache ‚Äî auto-generated | No |
| `.nuxt/` | Nuxt build cache ‚Äî auto-generated | No |

### MultiSelect Format

```
[x] .env ‚Äî Environment variables with secrets
[x] node_modules/ ‚Äî Dependencies (auto-installed)
[x] package-lock.json ‚Äî npm lock file (auto-generated)
[x] yarn.lock ‚Äî Yarn lock file (auto-generated)
[x] pnpm-lock.yaml ‚Äî pnpm lock file (auto-generated)
[x] dist/ ‚Äî Build output (auto-generated)
[x] build/ ‚Äî Build output (auto-generated)
[ ] .next/ ‚Äî Next.js build cache
[ ] .nuxt/ ‚Äî Nuxt build cache
```

### Detection Hints

- Show `package-lock.json` only if it exists or npm is the detected manager
- Show `yarn.lock` only if it exists or yarn is the detected manager
- Show `pnpm-lock.yaml` only if it exists or pnpm is the detected manager
- Show `.next/` only if Next.js is detected
- Show `.nuxt/` only if Nuxt is detected
- Always show `.env`, `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `build/`

---

## Go

Apply when the detected or stated language is Go.

| Pattern | Description | Recommended |
|---|---|---|
| `.env` | Environment variables with secrets | Yes |
| `go.sum` | Module checksum file ‚Äî auto-generated by `go mod tidy` | Yes |
| `vendor/` | Vendored dependencies ‚Äî auto-generated by `go mod vendor` | No |

### MultiSelect Format

```
[x] .env ‚Äî Environment variables with secrets
[x] go.sum ‚Äî Module checksums (auto-generated)
[ ] vendor/ ‚Äî Vendored dependencies (auto-generated)
```

---

## Rust

Apply when the detected or stated language is Rust.

| Pattern | Description | Recommended |
|---|---|---|
| `.env` | Environment variables with secrets | Yes |
| `Cargo.lock` | Cargo lock file ‚Äî auto-generated by `cargo build` | Yes |
| `target/` | Build output directory ‚Äî auto-generated by cargo | Yes |

### MultiSelect Format

```
[x] .env ‚Äî Environment variables with secrets
[x] Cargo.lock ‚Äî Lock file (auto-generated by cargo)
[x] target/ ‚Äî Build output (auto-generated)
```

---

## Combining Protection Presets

When presenting the multiSelect to the user, merge **General** presets with the relevant **stack-specific** presets into a single list. Order: General items first, then stack-specific items.

If the project uses multiple languages (e.g., Python backend + TypeScript frontend), combine presets from both stacks and deduplicate (`.env` appears once, not twice).

If the language is not covered by a specific preset section above, present only the General presets and add a free-text `AskUserQuestion`: "Are there any additional files or directories Claude should never edit?"

---

# Automation Presets (PostToolUse)

Post-edit automation hooks run formatters and linters on files after Claude modifies them. Each preset defines a tool, the file extensions it applies to, and the commands to run.

## Python

Apply when the detected or stated language is Python.

| Tool | File Extensions | Commands | Recommended |
|---|---|---|---|
| Ruff (format + lint) | `.py` | `ruff check --fix {file}`, `ruff format {file}` | Yes |
| Black (format only) | `.py` | `black {file}` | No |

### MultiSelect Format

```
[x] Ruff ‚Äî Auto-format and lint Python files (ruff check --fix + ruff format)
[ ] Black ‚Äî Auto-format Python files (black)
```

### Detection Hints

- Pre-check **Ruff** if `ruff` section exists in `pyproject.toml`, `.ruff.toml` exists, or `ruff` is in dev dependencies
- Pre-check **Black** if `black` section exists in `pyproject.toml` or `.black.toml` exists (but not if Ruff is also detected ‚Äî prefer Ruff)
- Show only one formatter as recommended. If both are detected, recommend Ruff (it supersedes Black)

---

## JavaScript / TypeScript

Apply when the detected or stated language is JavaScript or TypeScript.

| Tool | File Extensions | Commands | Recommended |
|---|---|---|---|
| Prettier | `.js`, `.ts`, `.jsx`, `.tsx`, `.css`, `.json` | `prettier --write {file}` | Yes |
| ESLint | `.js`, `.ts`, `.jsx`, `.tsx` | `eslint --fix {file}` | No |

### MultiSelect Format

```
[x] Prettier ‚Äî Auto-format JS/TS files (prettier --write)
[ ] ESLint ‚Äî Auto-fix JS/TS lint issues (eslint --fix)
```

### Detection Hints

- Pre-check **Prettier** if `.prettierrc`, `.prettierrc.json`, or `prettier` key in `package.json` exists
- Pre-check **ESLint** if `.eslintrc`, `eslint.config.*`, or `eslint` key in `package.json` exists
- Both can run together (Prettier for formatting, ESLint for lint rules)

---

## Go

Apply when the detected or stated language is Go.

| Tool | File Extensions | Commands | Recommended |
|---|---|---|---|
| gofmt | `.go` | `gofmt -w {file}` | Yes |

### MultiSelect Format

```
[x] gofmt ‚Äî Auto-format Go files (gofmt -w)
```

### Detection Hints

- Always pre-check ‚Äî `gofmt` ships with the Go toolchain and is the universal standard

---

## Rust

Apply when the detected or stated language is Rust.

| Tool | File Extensions | Commands | Recommended |
|---|---|---|---|
| rustfmt | `.rs` | `rustfmt {file}` | Yes |

### MultiSelect Format

```
[x] rustfmt ‚Äî Auto-format Rust files (rustfmt)
```

### Detection Hints

- Always pre-check ‚Äî `rustfmt` ships with the Rust toolchain via `rustup`

---

## Combining Automation Presets

Show only the presets relevant to the detected or stated stack. If the project uses multiple languages, combine presets from all relevant stacks.

If the language is not covered by a specific preset section above, ask a free-text `AskUserQuestion`: "Do you use a formatter or linter that should run automatically after Claude edits a file? If so, provide the command (e.g., `black {file}`)."
